preamble: The shift from the back unrounded vowel [ʌ] (the 'strut' vowel) to a mid-front unrounded vowel like [e] or [ε] involves fronting and raising. It is a hallmark of certain modern chain shifts where back vowels move forward to fill spaces left by other shifting vowels.

examples.note: Eckert documents STRUT shifting toward BET/BEN; quantitative evidence is less robust than for back vowel fronting in the CVS.
Fronting of the STRUT vowel is attested in younger Midland speakers as part of broader front vowel shifting.
